decision_note: promote strong historical medicinal plant family from review into the seed shortlist

supporting_works.title: The antiseptic or commonsense practice of medicine and guide to health : for the use of all true friends of medical science / by R.J. Parsons.

Our family physician : a plain, practical, and reliable guide to the detection and treatment of all the diseases common to this country : designed for use by people who have not made the practice of medicine a study or business, and who are not supposed to be familiar with technical phrases and names, but want information stated in plain English : gives, separately, with every disease, the treatment prescribed by each of the "schools" and full and explicit directions for nursing the sick, preparation of food for the sick, etc
supporting_works.creator: Stout, H. R. (Henry Rice), 1843-

supporting_works.title: The American practice of medicine: being a treatise on the character, causes, symptoms, morbid appearances, and treatment of the diseases of men, women, and children of all climates (Volume 1)
supporting_works.creator: Beach, W. (Wooster), 1794-1868.

supporting_works.title: Our family physician : a thoroughly reliable guide to the detection and treatment of all diseases that can be either checked in their career or treated entirely by an intelligent person, without the aid of a physician; especially such as require prompt and energetic measures, and those peculiar to this country. Embracing the allopathic, homeopathic, hydropathic, eclectic and herbal modes of treatment. Also giving full and explicit directions for nursing the sick,  preparation of food for the sick, etc. / by H. R. Stout.
The American family physician, or, Domestic guide to health : prepared expressly for the use of families, in language adapted to the understanding of the people : arranged in two divisions
supporting_works.creator: King, John, 1813-1893.

supporting_works.title: A handbook of family medicine and hygiene : together with descriptions of remedies, numerous choice formulas, dietary for the sick, rules for nursing, etc
supporting_works.creator: Cook, Wm. H. (William Henry), 1832-1899.

supporting_works.title: Beach's family physician and home guide for the treatment of the diseases of men, women and children, on reform principles
supporting_works.creator: Beach, W. (Wooster), 1794-1868.

supporting_works.title: The American practice condensed, or The family physician : being the scientific system of medicine
supporting_works.creator: Beach, W. (Wooster), 1794-1868.

supporting_works.title: A medical manual and medicine chest companion : for popular use in families and on ship board, for the treatment of the ordinary diseases of the human system / by Thomas Ritter.

Gunn's new family physician, or, Home book of health : forming a complete household guide, giving many valuable suggestions for avoiding disease and prolonging life, with plain directions in case of energency, and pointing out in familiar language the causes, symptoms, treatment and cure of diseases incident to men, women, and children with the simplest and best remedies : presenting a manual for nursing the sick, and describing minutely the properties and uses of hundreds of well-known medicinal plants : with supplementary treatises on anatomy, physiology, and hygiene, on domestic and sanitary economy, and on physical culture and development
supporting_works.creator: Gunn, John C.

overview.title: Domestic medicine , or, Medical vade mecum : a safe companion and guide for families, planters, commanders of ships or steamers, or any one who may require a true friend in time of need : this companion embraces, alphabetically arranged, the medicines in general use, the diseases and accidents of usual occurrence, the most useful articles of diet or drink for the sick or convalescent, with the best mode of preparing them, and numerous remarks in reference to bathing, exercise, and other hygienic measures to preserve health, to repair and strengthen an enervated constitution, and to cure disease
overview.section_heading: AND FOR SALE BY ALL BOOKSELLERS THROUGHOUT
overview.excerpt: A common and excellent remedy at the com- mencement of a cold, is a full dose of laudanum, one grain of opium, or ten to fifteen of Dover's powder, on going to bed, and in an hour to drink a tumblerful of hot lemonade...

retrieval_text: Boneset is a medicinal herb profile in the Herbalisti historical corpus. It is represented across 83 works and 239 source-linked chunks. Recorded variants include Boneset. Common plant-part co-mentions include Root, Leaf, Bark, Flower, Seed, Gum. Common preparation co-mentions include Tea, Infusion, Powder, Emetic, Tincture, Pill. Common condition co-mentions include Cold or catarrh, Fever, Vomiting or nausea, Cough, Inflammation, Asthma. Caution co-mentions include Dose caution, Narcotic or sedative caution, Allergy or sensitivity caution, Bleeding caution, Child-use caution, Toxicity watch. Representative source works include The antiseptic or commonsense practice of medicine and guide to health : for the use of all true friends of medical science / by R.J. Parsons.; Our family physician : a plain, practical, and reliable guide to the detection and treatment of all the diseases common to this country : designed for use by people who have not made the practice of medicine a study or business, and who are not supposed to be familiar with technical phrases and names, but want information stated in plain English : gives, separately, with every disease, the treatment prescribed by each of the "schools" and full and explicit directions for nursing the sick, preparation of food for the sick, etc; The American practice of medicine: being a treatise on the character, causes, symptoms, morbid appearances, and treatment of the diseases of men, women, and children of all climates (Volume 1); Our family physician : a thoroughly reliable guide to the detection and treatment of all diseases that can be either checked in their career or treated entirely by an intelligent person, without the aid of a physician; especially such as require prompt and energetic measures, and those peculiar to this country. Embracing the allopathic, homeopathic, hydropathic, eclectic and herbal modes of treatment. Also giving full and explicit directions for nursing the sick,  preparation of food for the sick, etc. / by H. R. Stout.. This is a retrieval summary of historical co-mentions only, not a medical claim.
